title: Bind Bureau CLI execution to an observable current source identity
goal: Prevent `python3 -m bureau.cli` or the `bureau` command from silently executing a stale globally installed package while reading a newer Registry checkout, and expose the exact code, Registry and state-store identities in every operational response.

acceptance.assertion: Every Bureau CLI JSON response exposes module path, package version, source commit or immutable release digest, Registry root/head/dirty state and state-store schema identity.
A deployed package that is older or incompatible with the selected Registry fails normal claims and writes with a typed stale-runtime result while retaining read-only diagnosis.
A single documented and machine-discoverable launcher selects the deployed immutable Bureau runtime or the current checkout explicitly; ambient user-site imports cannot silently win.
Bureau deployment and upgrade produce an immutable manifest, version/hash readback and rollback path comparable to Grabowski runtime identity.
Tests reproduce a stale user-site package, editable checkout, detached worktree, incompatible schema, clean deployed runtime and explicit source override.

observation.effect: The stale package reported lifecycle mismatches already fixed in origin/main and blocked normal Bureau coordination with code/Registry drift.

validation.full_local_suite: Complete Bureau pytest suite reached 100 percent with empty stderr on both reviewed heads.
validation.focused_tests: Runtime identity, dirty/stale/unbound gates, immutable installer, rollback, manifest drift, package-tree drift, existing relative launcher symlink migration and systemd unit contracts passed.
validation.lint: Ruff passed on all changed Python files.
validation.systemd: systemd-analyze --user verify passed for the reviewed reference units.

ambient_user_site.direct_module_probe: python3 -m bureau.cli now fails loudly with ModuleNotFoundError instead of loading stale user-site code

live_proof.broad_repo_probe_boundary: A broad repo lease remains intentionally forbidden and is not a runtime-health test.

verified_acceptance.source-identity: Verified live: the canonical JSON envelope exposes immutable module, manifest and package-tree hashes, Registry head/origin/dirty state, state path/schema/integrity and explicit non-claims.
verified_acceptance.stale-runtime-gate: Verified by tests for ambient, dirty, stale, unbound and package/manifest drift; mutation fails closed while bounded diagnostics remain available.
verified_acceptance.canonical-launcher: Verified by the installed /home/alex/.local/bin/bureau launcher. The stale ambient user-site package is quarantined, so python3 -m bureau.cli fails loudly rather than silently loading old code.
verified_acceptance.deployment: Verified by immutable release d85ad423aa02-src72aaf70b2218, manifest/package/launcher hashes, rollback metadata, install receipt and independent live readback.
verified_acceptance.tests: Verified by focused positive and negative tests, complete local pytest runs, green Ruff, valid systemd reference units and green Python 3.10/3.12 CI on both implementation PRs.

metadata.verification_note: PRs #521 and #522 are merged; the exact merge commit is deployed to the canonical manifest runtime and Grabowski contract venv; stale ambient imports are quarantined; a final exact-scope Grabowski lease contract returned no findings and all component hashes matched.
